completion_summary: Delivered a machine-verifiable scientific-readiness audit that separates executable pipeline status from defensible benchmark claims. The audit records 31.4% weighted readiness, 13/41 checks passed, and 18 blockers, including 4/6 stale release task hashes, invalid oracle references on two tasks, underpowered five-seed comparisons, unstable held-out rankings, missing RL/real-LLM/expanded-AL evidence, missing axis-level OOD/private/exploit evidence, pending vNext integration, and incomplete release/paper artifacts. Added a fail-closed --require-ready mode, tests, literature-calibrated workstream guidance, and a ten-package claim queue. Full local release gate passed with 889 tests passed and 11 optional reference tests skipped.
notes: Audit-only workstream. No edits to tasks, scenarios, runtime, scoring, agents, configs, release bundles, public docs, paper artifacts, or existing evidence. Follow-up implementation and experiment packages require separate claims after this gap report is accepted.
scope: Build a machine-verifiable repository-wide audit of ChemWorld serious benchmark completeness and scientific meaningfulness, covering claim definition, per-task validity, baseline statistics, generalization and anti-gaming, runtime/backend evidence, scoring/replay provenance, release reproducibility, and readiness for optimization/RL/LLM comparisons; update the maintainer checklist without changing environment behavior or frozen benchmark semantics.
